concerns.text: SC-002 states: 'The reduction in inference latency... is measured against the improvement target... defined in the research hypothesis'. The text cuts off mid-sentence and fails to specify the reference value or the metric definition (e.g., 'measured against a 40% reduction target' or 'measured against the baseline latency recorded in the FastContext run'). Without a specific reference or threshold, this success criterion is unmeasurable.

SC-003 states: 'The token consumption... is measured against a predefined variance threshold'. The term 'predefined variance threshold' is not defined in the spec, nor is a specific value or reference provided. A success criterion cannot be verified if the threshold against which it is measured is not explicitly stated or linked to a concrete definition in the document.

SC-004 states: 'The performance gap... is measured against the "sharp degradation" expectation'. The phrase 'sharp degradation' is qualitative and undefined. To be measurable, this must specify a quantitative threshold (e.g., 'measured against a 10% precision drop threshold') or a specific statistical condition defined in the methodology.
